Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Standard P2P sequence 1 Create Purchase Requisition 2 Approve Purchase Requisition Approval must happen before PO issuance 3 Issue Purchase Order PO sent to vendor after approval 4 Receive Goods Goods receipt happens after PO is issued and vendor delivers 5 Vendor Invoice Received Invoice arrives typically after goods received or around same time 6 Match Invoice to PO Three way match PO Goods Receipt Invoice 7 Release Payment Payment after matching verification 8 Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1001 Create PR Approve PR OK after creation Issue PO OK after approval Receive Goods OK after PO Vendor Invoice Received OK after goods Match Invoice to PO OK after both invoice and goods received Release Payment OK after matching Close Case OK This looks like a normal flow Timeline makes sense

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 Create PR Issue PO Wait that is 10 minutes later but no approval yet Vendor Invoice Received Release Payment Payment before approval of PR And before receiving goods Approve Purchase Requisition Approval after PO issued AND after payment released Receive Goods After payment Match Invoice to PO After payment

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let me re read carefully 1002 2024 02 01 08 30 Create Purchase Requisition 1002 2024 02 01 08 40 Issue Purchase Order 1002 2024 02 03 07 00 Vendor Invoice Received 1002 2024 02 03 07 15 Release Payment 1002 2024 02 03 08 00 Approve Purchase Requisition 1002 2024 02 04 09 00 Receive Goods 1002 2024 02 05 08 00 Match Invoice to PO Yes The approval happens on Feb 3 but the PO was issued on Feb 1

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is a major anomaly issuing a PO before the requisition is approved Also payment is released on Feb 3 at 07 15 but the approval happens at 08 00 on the same day and goods are received on Feb 4 after payment Also matching happens after payment which is wrong

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Create PR Approve PR OK Issue PO OK Release Payment Wait this is before receiving goods and before receiving invoice Vendor Invoice Received Invoice after payment Receive Goods Goods after payment Match Invoice to PO After payment Close Case

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is a case of Payment in advance or Prepayment but in a standard P2P this is highly unusual and risky It could be fraud or error

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Create PR Approve PR OK Receive Goods Wait before PO is issued Issue PO PO issued after goods received Vendor Invoice Received OK relative to PO Match Invoice to PO OK Release Payment OK Close Case

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This suggests Maverick buying or Off contract purchasing where goods are received without a formal PO in place This breaks the three way match concept and proper procurement controls

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Summary of anomalies Case 1002 1 PO issued before PR approval 2 Payment released before PR approval 3 Payment released before goods receipt 4 Payment released before invoice matching 5 Invoice matching after payment should be before

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003 1 Payment released before goods receipt 2 Payment released before invoice receipt 3 Payment released before invoice matching This is Pay on Account or advance payment without verification

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1004 1 Goods received before PO issued This indicates receiving goods without a valid PO which is a control weakness

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Key concepts to mention Segregation of duties Three way match PO Goods Receipt Invoice Approval authority PO should not be issued without approval Payment controls should not pay before verifying receipt of goods services Financial risk paying for goods not received duplicate payments fraud Compliance with procurement policies

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 looks clean Case 1002 definitely PO before approval and payment before approval Case 1003 payment way before goods invoice Case 1004 goods 15 minutes before PO Could be data entry error but still an anomaly
